item.title: July Joddha Sangsad demands justice over memorial torching
item.description: Bangladesh July Joddha Sangsad on Tuesday demanded swift action against those responsible for setting fire to and vandalising the country’s first July martyrs’ memorial in Narayanganj....

item.title: BB allows NBL to lease HQ unit to raise income
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Tuesday allowed crisis-hit National Bank to commercially lease its under-construction headquarters in Dhaka’s Panthapath, granting a rare exemption from banking law to help the lender create a new source of income amid severe financial stress....

item.title: Korean envoy meets PM’s defence adviser
item.description: South Korean ambassador to Bangladesh Kim Ji-Joon on Tuesday paid a courtesy call on prime minister’s defence adviser retired Brigadier General AKM Shamsul Islam at the armed forces division headquarters in Dhaka Cantonment....Maps

item.title: ICT probe names Hasina, 40 others over Shapla Chattar crackdown
item.description: The Investigation Agency of the International Crimes Tribunal on Tuesday submitted its probe report to the chief prosecutor, naming Sheikh Hasina and 40 others over the killing of Hefazat-e-Islam activists in 2013 Shapla Chattar crackdown....

item.title: Scam losses in Asian regions reach $114b: UN
item.description: Victims of transnational online scams in East Asia, Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand lost up to $114.1 billion last year, a UN report said on Tuesday, with estimated losses at least tripling from 2023....News

item.title: Iran steps up retaliation against US
item.description: Iran stepped up its strikes in the Middle East on Tuesday, two weeks after its war with the United States resumed, with Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain coming under attacks....

item.title: US readies new tariffs as Trump’s 10pc global levy to expire
item.description: The United States is acting soon on a host of new tariffs targeting dozens of countries, trade envoy Jamieson Greer said Tuesday, with president Donald Trump’s temporary global levy due to expire this week....

item.title: Milestone tragedy victims, families want justice, compensation
item.description: One year after the Milestone School tragedy, victims, bereaved families and teachers on Tuesday demanded justice, adequate compensation, publication of the official investigation report and health cards to ensure continued treatment for the injured....

item.title: MILESTONE TRAGEDY ANNIVERSARY: Victims, families want justice, compensation
item.description: One year after the Milestone School tragedy, victims, bereaved families and teachers on Tuesday demanded justice, adequate compensation, publication of the official investigation report and health cards to ensure continued treatment for the injured....

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza
item.description: Gaza’s civil defence agency and witnesses said Israeli strikes killed 12 people, including a family of six, in the Palestinian territory on Tuesday......

item.title: Rain dampens search for dozens missing in Afghanistan flood
item.description: Heavy rain and warnings of further flooding dampened the search Tuesday for dozens of residents missing in eastern Afghanistan, after a major deluge smashed through one province killing at least 23 people......

item.title: Floods spread
item.description: The onrush of water from upstream across the border continued to swell rivers, leading to the inundation of low-lying parts of the country’s central, north and northeastern regions on Tuesday....

item.title: Lebanon deploys army in south after Israel deal
item.description: The Lebanese army announced on Tuesday that it had begun deploying to an area in the south, one of three ‘pilot zones’ outlined in a deal with Israel to disarm Hezbollah......

item.title: Burnham cuts energy tax, tells ministers to cut living costs
item.description: Andy Burnham told the inaugural meeting of his cabinet Tuesday that he would lead a ‘cost-of-livinggovernment’, after unveiling an energy tax cut on his first full day as prime minister......Government

item.title: PNSB new president Prof Shirin, GS Saimul
item.description: The Paediatric Nephrology Society of Bangladesh has announced a new 33-member executive committee to lead the organisation’s initiatives in Bangladesh....

item.title: UN signals major rise in Ukraine-Russia war civilian deaths
item.description: The United Nations said Tuesday that civilian casualties in the war raging between Russia and Ukraine rose 37 per cent in the first half of this year......

item.title: Philippines, China escalate sea dispute
item.description: The Philippines and China summoned each other’s ambassadors on Tuesday following a clash at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, which sparked condemnation of Beijing by Manila’s allies......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Govt step must to protect rape victim’s identity
item.description: MAKING public the identity of victims of rape and sexual violence and of their families is illegal as it doubly victimises the victims. Yet this continues at the hands of media houses, print and broadcast. Digital content creators and bloggers on social media platforms and YouTube channels have pushed it a bit further by making public not only the identity of the victims and their......Maps

item.title: No damages, criminal case in Milestone disaster worrying
item.description: THE government response has been nothing short of unconscionable to the disaster at the Milestone School and College even a year after as compensation has been paid and no case filed. An Air Force fighter jet crashed into institution at Uttara in Dhaka, killing 38 people, including 28 students, mostly primary school children, and injuring 63 others. The victims and their......

item.title: From early warning to early action
item.description: BANGLADESH has earned global recognition for its disaster preparedness, but the monsoon floods between July 5 and July 12 this year exposed a critical gap: early warning without early action is no longer enough. A monsoon depression over the Bay of Bengal triggered torrential rain, flash floods, river swelling and landslides across ten districts in the southeast and......

item.title: World’s unfinished promise
item.description: I HAVE visited the Rohingya refugee camps in Cox’s Bazar many times over the years. Every visit leaves me with the same question: what will become of this generation if the world continues to see them only as recipients of humanitarian aid?......City& Local Guides

item.title: What to do with secularism and state religion?
item.description: DOES a state need a religion? More specifically, why does a Muslim-majority country need to declare Islam its state religion? The reverse question is equally important. What would change if secularism disappeared from Bangladesh’s fundamental principles of state policy?......

item.title: City that cannot drain
item.description: IT NO longer takes a torrential downpour to bring Dhaka to a halt. Half an hour of moderate rainfall is often enough to submerge roads in Mirpur, Dhanmondi, Badda and Karwan Bazar, turning routine commutes into hours-long ordeals. Vehicles stall, businesses lose customers, students miss classes and ambulances struggle through traffic. What was once regarded as......

item.title: BNP activist hacked to death at Sabujbagh
item.description: A local BNP activist was hacked to death in an alleged attack stemming from a previous dispute in the capital's Sabujbagh area on Tuesday night....News

item.title: Court orders DNA test for 2 more suspects in Tonu murder case
item.description: A Senior Judicial Magistrate Court in Cumilla has ordered DNA test of two more suspects in connection with the alleged rape and murder of college student and cultural activist Sohagi Jahan Tonu a decade ago....

item.title: BMU seminar stresses early detection to combat oral cancer
item.description: A central seminar on oral cancer prevention, surgical advances and therapeutic innovations was held at Bangladesh Medical University in Dhaka on Monday, highlighting the importance of early detection, multidisciplinary treatment and greater public awareness....

item.title: Apollo Hospitals Chennai holds int’l CME prog in Dhaka
item.description: Apollo Hospitals Chennai, in collaboration with Apollo Clinic Dhanmondi, Dhaka [Licensee: JMI Specialised Hospital PLC], organised an International Continuing Medical Education Programme for doctors at Apollo Clinic Dhanmondi on Sunday....

item.title: Technical glitch reduces LNG supply by 450 mmcfd
item.description: The supply of regasified liquefied natural gas from the Moheshkhali LNG terminal in Cox’s Bazar dropped by about 450 million cubic feet per day on Tuesday due to a technical glitch....Politics(Right)

item.title: Taher killing day observed
item.description: Different factions of Jatiya Samajtantrik Dal observed Colonel Taher killing day on Tuesday, commemorating the 50th anniversary of the freedom fighter’s execution in 1976....
